description_long: This sculpture depicts two children riding a horse, with one child sitting behind the other and raising an object, possibly a canteen or a ball, high above their head. The horse stands on a textured base that mimics rough terrain, its head lowered as if drinking or grazing. The children are rendered with detailed clothing and expressive postures, capturing a sense of adventure or exploration. The entire piece is cast in a dark, metallic material, likely bronze, with a glossy finish that highlights the intricate modeling of the figures and the horse's musculature. The sculpture is life-sized or slightly smaller, and the base is integral to the composition, anchoring the figures in a naturalistic setting. The work exudes a sense of narrative and movement, inviting viewers to imagine the story behind the scene.
description: A detailed bronze sculpture of two children riding a horse, with one child raising an object overhead, all set on a textured base. The figures are lifelike and expressive, with a glossy dark finish.
description_view_0: This view shows the left side of the horse and both children, with the child in the rear clearly raising an object and the horse's head lowered.
description_view_1: From the rear, both children are visible on the horse's back, and the raised object is prominent, with the horse's tail and back legs in view.
description_view_2: The right side of the sculpture is visible, showing the children in profile and the horse's head turned slightly downward.
description_view_3: A frontal view of the horse and children, with the raised object directly above and the horse's face and chest visible.
attribute_subject_matter: two children riding a horse, one raising an object
attribute_location_context: No specific setting is visible; the sculpture appears isolated against a plain background.
